title: Rooftop-Garden Water-Stress Scaling Law
strategies: constant / mean-of-train law -> reproduces the internal baseline (trivial, ~0.10)
single product power law L=k*A^c1*W^c2 by log-linear fit (no floor, no resource pooling) -> trivial baseline
additive first-order model L=a+b/A+c/W (right monotonicity + a floor, but separate reciprocals not a pooled budget) -> greedy
correct combined-resource form L=Linf+C*(A+rho*W)^(-p), with (rho,p) chosen by an internal train/val split to extrapolate -> strong
over-parameterised polynomial that memorises train noise -> penalised + extrapolates poorly
notes: Format-E symbolic regression skinned as a rooftop garden. The solver sees a noisy (bed area A m^2, daily water W L/day, canopy water-stress index L) table from small pilot beds and must emit a closed-form law that EXTRAPOLATES to a hidden larger-rooftop region (bigger A and W), regenerated deterministically inside the checker. Hidden law L=Linf+C*(A+rho*W)^(-p): an irreducible stress floor plus a single power decay in a POOLED effective-resource budget (A+rho*W), where rho is the litres-to-area equivalence -- a combined-resource scaling law distinct from separated additive-power forms. Coefficients (Linf,C,rho,p) and multiplicative-noise level vary per testId. Score = baseline_rmse/held_out_rmse (minimisation) with a gentle complexity penalty; irreducible held-out noise keeps strong below 1.0. Deterministic CPU only; no wall-time, no shipped groundtruth module, no law/seed printed by gen.py.
